questions.q: What led to the decline of the Congress System in India?
questions.answer: Rise of regional parties and issues of governance led to the decline of the Congress System in India.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Swatantra Party and what was its main ideology?
questions.answer: C. Rajagopalachari was the leader of the Swatantra Party. Its main ideology was free enterprise and limited government intervention.

questions.q: Explain the factors responsible for the rise of the Bharatiya Jana Sangh (BJS). (10 marks)
questions.answer: The factors responsible for the rise of the Bharatiya Jana Sangh (BJS) include the decline of the Congress System, the rise of Hindu nationalism, and the influence of the RSS.

questions.q: What was the impact of the rise of regional parties on the Congress System?
questions.answer: The rise of regional parties led to a decline in the Congress System's dominance and power.

questions.q: Who founded the Praja Socialist Party (PSP) and what were its main goals?
questions.answer: Asaf Ali founded the Praja Socialist Party (PSP), and its main goals were land reforms and social justice.

questions.q: Explain the concept of the 'Congress System' and its significance in Indian politics. (15 marks)
questions.answer: The Congress System refers to the dominance of the Indian National Congress in Indian politics. Its significance lies in its ability to bring together diverse groups and interests, providing a platform for different voices to be heard.

questions.q: What was the impact of the Emergency on the Congress System?
questions.answer: The Emergency led to a decline in the Congress System's popularity and credibility.

questions.q: Who was the leader of the Bharatiya Lok Dal and what was its main ideology?
questions.answer: Charan Singh was the leader of the Bharatiya Lok Dal, and its main ideology was socialism and anti-Congressism.

questions.q: Explain the factors responsible for the decline of the Congress System in the 1960s. (10 marks)
questions.answer: The factors responsible for the decline of the Congress System in the 1960s include the rise of regional parties, the decline of the Congress's ideological appeal, and the impact of the Emergency.

questions.q: What was the impact of the rise of the Janata Party on the Congress System?
questions.answer: The rise of the Janata Party led to a significant decline in the Congress System's dominance and power.
